mappings.rationale: Directly covers the art of motion pictures, including the roles of director, writer, editor, and the creative process — core topics of this section.
Directly covers the historical development of motion pictures as an art form, relevant to the section's full scope.
Covers the Hollywood film industry, directly relevant to the section's treatment of the motion-picture industry (section C.1) and Western cinema history.
Covers film noir as a major motion-picture genre, relevant to the classification of motion pictures and their artistic conventions.
Covers early cinema before sound, relevant to the section's treatment of the history and technology of motion pictures.
Covers documentary as a classification of motion pictures, relevant to section A.1 and to the use of film as education and propaganda (section C.2).
Directly covers the interrelation of music with motion pictures, explicitly addressed in section B.
Covers a major non-Western cinema tradition, relevant to the section's treatment of the motion-picture industry and motion-picture art globally.
Covers French cinema, a major artistic tradition in motion pictures, relevant to the section's treatment of film as art and the history of cinema.
Covers British cinema, a significant tradition in motion pictures, relevant to the section's treatment of film art and cinema history.
Photography is a foundational technology and artistic component of cinema, relevant to the section's treatment of the camera and technology in the creative process.
Music is one of the arts interrelated with motion pictures, as explicitly covered in section B.
Relevant to the section's treatment of scenic design, costumes, and the visual design elements of motion-picture production (section A.3).
Relevant to the section's treatment of the motion-picture industry and the use of film as a medium of propaganda (section C.2).
